title: NCEA Level 3 Mechanics – Practice Paper 2
instructions: Answer ALL questions. Show all working for short and long answer questions. Calculators are permitted. g = 9.81 m/s².

questions.questionText: A 4 kg box is pushed across a frictionless surface with a net force of 12 N. What is its acceleration?
questions.explanation: Using Newton's second law: a = F_net / m = 12 / 4 = 3 m/s².

questions.questionText: A 3 kg object falls from rest through 10 m (ignore air resistance). What is its kinetic energy just before impact? (g = 10 m/s²)
questions.explanation: All gravitational PE converts to KE. GPE = mgh = 3 × 10 × 10 = 300 J.

questions.questionText:
A mass of 0.4 kg oscillates on a spring with spring constant k = 100 N/m and amplitude 0.05 m.
(a) Calculate the angular frequency of the oscillation.
(b) Calculate the period of oscillation.
(c) Calculate the maximum speed of the mass.
questions.explanation: Standard SHM spring-mass problem. Use ω = √(k/m), T = 2π/ω, and v_max = Aω.

questions.questionText:
A solid disk of mass 2 kg and radius 0.3 m is initially at rest. A constant torque of 6 N·m is applied for 4 seconds.
(a) Calculate the moment of inertia of the disk.
(b) Calculate the angular acceleration.
(c) Calculate the final angular velocity.
(d) Calculate the final rotational kinetic energy.
questions.explanation: Rotational mechanics problem. Use I = ½MR² for a solid disk, τ = Iα for angular acceleration, ω = αt for final angular velocity.
